mnemonics: KISS: Keep It Simplex, Stupid! (For the Simplex Method)
PENALTY: Penalty and Barrier Methods are like pushing a boundary.
GRADIENT DESCENT: Go Downhill Rapidly And Don't Ever Climb Steep Terrain.
keyPoints: The Simplex Method is an iterative procedure for solving linear programming problems by moving along the edges of the feasible region to the optimal vertex.
Duality Theory states that every linear programming problem has an associated dual problem; the solution to one gives information about the solution to the other.
Kuhn-Tucker Conditions are first-order necessary conditions for a solution in nonlinear programming to be optimal, provided some regularity conditions are satisfied.
commonMistakes: Misinterpreting slack/surplus variables, leading to an incorrect initial simplex tableau.
Forgetting to check the second-order conditions (concavity/convexity) when using Kuhn-Tucker conditions, which are only necessary but not sufficient for optimality.
Improperly handling constraints in penalty and barrier methods, resulting in a solution that does not converge to the true optimum or becomes numerically unstable.
